title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the Joy Cafe, Samuel Kid informs Poirot that he recognized a mysterious woman fleeing the scene, though he cannot identify her name or specific details beyond her cultured appearance. Following this, Inspector Stanley Bill arrives accompanied by Henry Nigges, the brother of the deceased Richard Nigges.

Poirot questions Henry regarding the other murder victims, Harriet Seppel and Edith Gransberry. Henry reveals that while he never met Edith, he knew of her through correspondence because she was once engaged to Richard. Henry explains that Richard had been a lawyer in Pigeon Valley who lived in Glebeholme until 1913. At that time, Richard broke off his engagement with Ada Gransberry and moved to Devonshire to live with Henry and Henry's wife, Clara.

Henry notes that Richard hated Glebeholme and became a reclusive, depressed man after his move, spending his time drinking alone and refusing social or religious engagement; he even rejected a Bible gifted by Henry's family. However, Henry observes that a few months before his death, Richard’s spirits seemed to lift. He had improved his hygiene, stopped drinking, and appeared to be planning something with a new goal. Henry expresses the impression that Richard had lived like a "dead man" for years and suggests that Richard may have even known he would be murdered.
